chunks.title: Judah's Victory at Bezek
chunks.reason: Narrative of Judah and Simeon's campaign against the Canaanites and the defeat of Adoni-bezek.

chunks.title: Capture of Jerusalem and Debir
chunks.reason: Account of Judah taking Jerusalem and Caleb's reward to Othniel for capturing Debir.

chunks.title: Kenites and Final Conquests of Judah
chunks.reason: Description of the Kenites joining Judah and the completion of Judah's territorial gains.

chunks.title: Tribal Successes and Failures
chunks.reason: Catalog of the remaining tribes' partial successes or failures in driving out the Canaanites.

chunks.title: The Angel's Rebuke at Bochim
chunks.reason: A continuous narrative unit containing the angel's speech and the people's immediate reaction.

chunks.title: Death of Joshua and a New Generation
chunks.reason: A complete narrative section describing the transition from Joshua's leadership to a generation that forgot God.

chunks.title: The Cycle of Sin and Judgment
chunks.reason: A cohesive theological argument detailing the pattern of Israel's apostasy, Yahweh's judgment, the raising of judges, and the final conclusion regarding the remaining nations.

chunks.title: Introduction to the Nations and Israel's Sin
chunks.reason: Establishes the purpose of remaining nations and describes Israel's initial apostasy.

chunks.title: Othniel Delivers Israel
chunks.reason: First cycle of oppression and deliverance through Othniel.

chunks.title: Ehud Delivers Israel from Moab
chunks.reason: Second cycle of oppression and deliverance through Ehud's assassination of Eglon.

chunks.title: Shamgar Delivers Israel
chunks.reason: Brief account of Shamgar's victory over the Philistines.

chunks.title: Israel's Oppression under Jabin
chunks.reason: Establishes the historical context of Israel's sin and oppression by King Jabin and Sisera.

chunks.title: Deborah Summons Barak
chunks.reason: Introduces Deborah as judge and records the dialogue where she calls Barak to battle.

chunks.title: Mobilization of Forces
chunks.reason: Describes the gathering of troops and Sisera's response to the threat.

chunks.title: The Battle at Mount Tabor
chunks.reason: Depicts the divine intervention and military victory over Sisera's army.

chunks.title: Death of Sisera by Jael
chunks.reason: Narrates the flight of Sisera and his assassination by Jael in her tent.

chunks.title: Final Subjugation of Canaan
chunks.reason: Concludes the account with the total defeat of King Jabin.

chunks.title: Opening Praise to Yahweh
chunks.reason: Deborah and Barak begin the song with a call to praise God for His majestic intervention in history.

chunks.title: Description of Israel's Distress and Call to Action
chunks.reason: The singer describes the chaos before the victory and invites the people to remember God's deeds.

chunks.title: Call to Arms and Tribal Roll Call
chunks.reason: A direct address to Deborah and Barak followed by a detailed list of which tribes participated and which hesitated.

chunks.title: The Battle at Megiddo
chunks.reason: A narrative account of the military conflict, divine intervention through nature, and the cursing of Meroz.

chunks.title: The Death of Sisera by Jael
chunks.reason: A detailed narrative focusing on Jael's hospitality and her killing of the enemy commander.

chunks.title: Final Benediction
chunks.reason: The song concludes with a prayer for God's enemies to perish and His lovers to shine like the sun.

chunks.title: Israel's Oppression and Cry for Help
chunks.reason: Describes the seven-year oppression by Midian and Israel's subsequent cry to Yahweh.

chunks.title: The Prophet's Oracle of Rebuke
chunks.reason: A prophet delivers a message from Yahweh recounting past deliverance and current disobedience.

chunks.title: Gideon's Call and the Sign of Fire
chunks.reason: The angel calls Gideon, who requests a sign with fire from the rock before building an altar.

chunks.title: Gideon Destroys the Altar of Baal
chunks.reason: Gideon destroys his father's altar by night and earns the name Jerubbaal after a confrontation.

chunks.title: The Enemy Assembles and Gideon Rallies Troops
chunks.reason: Midianite forces camp in Jezreel while the Spirit of Yahweh empowers Gideon to gather an army.

chunks.title: Gideon's Fleece Tests
chunks.reason: Gideon speaks with God twice to confirm the promise of victory using a wool fleece.

chunks.title: Selection of the Army
chunks.reason: Describes Gideon reducing his army from 32,000 to 300 men based on Yahweh's instructions at the spring.

chunks.title: Gideon's Night Visit
chunks.reason: Narrates Gideon going to the Midianite camp with Purah and hearing a dream that confirms victory.

chunks.title: The Battle at Harod
chunks.reason: Details the tactical deployment of the 300 men, their surprise attack with trumpets and pitchers, and the resulting rout.

chunks.title: Pursuit and Capture of Princes
chunks.reason: Describes the pursuit by Israelite tribes and the capture of the Midianite princes Oreb and Zeeb.

chunks.title: Ephraim's Complaint and Gideon's Reply
chunks.reason: A complete dialogue unit where Ephraim confronts Gideon and he responds with a diplomatic argument that resolves the tension.

chunks.title: Pursuit of Midian Kings and Retribution at Succoth and Penuel
chunks.reason: A continuous narrative sequence covering the pursuit across the Jordan, the interaction with Succoth and Penuel, the battle at Karkor, and the subsequent vengeance.

chunks.title: Execution of Zebah and Zalmunna
chunks.reason: A focused dialogue scene between Gideon and the captured kings regarding their victims at Tabor, culminating in their execution.

chunks.title: Offer of Kingship and the Ephod
chunks.reason: A mixed unit containing a speech offering kingship, Gideon's argument rejecting it, his request for gold, and the narrative result of the ephod becoming a snare.

chunks.title: Gideon's Death and Israel's Apostasy
chunks.reason: A concluding narrative block covering Gideon's family details, his death, burial, and the immediate spiritual decline of Israel after his passing.

chunks.title: Abimelech Becomes King
chunks.reason: Narrative of Abimelech's rise to power through his mother's relatives and the killing of his brothers.

chunks.title: Jotham's Parable and Curse
chunks.reason: Jotham delivers a parable of the trees followed by a prophetic curse on Shechem and Abimelech.

chunks.title: God's Judgment Begins
chunks.reason: Narrative describing God sending an evil spirit to cause conflict between Abimelech and the Shechemites.

chunks.title: Gaal vs. Zebul
chunks.reason: Dialogue and confrontation between Gaal and the city ruler Zebul regarding Abimelech's rule.

chunks.title: Siege of Shechem
chunks.reason: Narrative of Abimelech's military campaign to capture and destroy the city of Shechem.

chunks.title: The Tower of Shechem
chunks.reason: Narrative of the lords of the tower seeking refuge and their subsequent destruction by fire.

chunks.title: Death of Abimelech
chunks.reason: Narrative of the siege of Thebez and Abimelech's death by a millstone.

chunks.title: Conclusion of the Story
chunks.reason: Narrative conclusion summarizing God's judgment on Abimelech and the men of Shechem.

chunks.title: Judges Tola and Jair
chunks.reason: Narrative summary of two judges' reigns and deaths.

chunks.title: Israel's Apostasy and Oppression
chunks.reason: Description of Israel's sin and subsequent military defeat by Philistines and Ammonites.

chunks.title: Israel's Repentance and Yahweh's Rebuke
chunks.reason: Exchange of dialogue between Israel and Yahweh regarding their sin and deliverance.

chunks.title: Mobilization in Gilead
chunks.reason: Narrative of armies gathering and the princes seeking a leader.

chunks.title: Jephthah's Exile
chunks.reason: Introduces Jephthah's background and his expulsion from his family.

chunks.title: Jephthah Called to Leadership
chunks.reason: The elders recruit Jephthah and he negotiates his terms of command.

chunks.title: Jephthah's Diplomatic Note
chunks.reason: A detailed historical and legal argument presented to the Ammonite king.

chunks.title: The Battle with Ammon
chunks.reason: Jephthah is empowered by the Spirit and defeats the enemy.

chunks.title: The Vow Fulfilled
chunks.reason: Jephthah returns home, meets his daughter, and fulfills his vow.

chunks.title: Conflict with Ephraim
chunks.reason: A complete narrative unit covering the dispute, battle, and execution at the Jordan fords.

chunks.title: Succession of Judges
chunks.reason: A structured list detailing the reigns and deaths of Jephthah, Ibzan, Elon, and Abdon.

chunks.title: Israel's Oppression and Manoah's Family
chunks.reason: Sets the historical context of Philistine oppression and introduces the barren couple.

chunks.title: Angel Appears to the Woman
chunks.reason: Records the first divine announcement of Samson's birth and his Nazirite vows to the mother.

chunks.title: Manoah Prays and Receives Instruction
chunks.reason: Describes Manoah's prayer for clarification and the angel's reiteration of the dietary laws.

chunks.title: The Sacrifice and Divine Revelation
chunks.reason: Narrates the meal preparation, the miraculous ascent of the angel, and the couple's realization of God's presence.

chunks.title: Birth and Empowerment of Samson
chunks.reason: Concludes the narrative with the birth of the child and the initial stirring of the Spirit.

chunks.title: Samson's Choice of Wife
chunks.reason: Describes Samson's decision to marry a Philistine woman and the divine purpose behind it.

chunks.title: The Lion and the Honey
chunks.reason: Narrates Samson killing a lion and discovering honey in its carcass.

chunks.title: The Riddle at the Feast
chunks.reason: Details the wedding feast where Samson proposes a riddle to the Philistines.

chunks.title: The Riddle Solved
chunks.reason: Describes the wife's pressure on Samson and the Philistines solving the riddle.

chunks.title: Samson's Vengeance
chunks.reason: Concludes with Samson striking down thirty men and his wife being given to another.

chunks.title: Samson's Revenge on the Philistines
chunks.reason: A complete narrative unit covering Samson's conflict with his father-in-law and his subsequent retaliation against the Philistines.

chunks.title: Samson Delivered at Lehi
chunks.reason: A continuous narrative describing the Judahites binding Samson, his release by the Spirit, and his victory with the jawbone.

chunks.title: Samson's Thirst and Final Years
chunks.reason: A concluding narrative segment detailing Samson's prayer for water, the miracle at En-hakkore, and his period of judging Israel.

chunks.title: Samson at Gaza
chunks.reason: A complete narrative unit describing Samson's first visit to Gaza and his feat of carrying the city gates.

chunks.title: Samson and Delilah
chunks.reason: A continuous narrative block detailing the repeated attempts by Delilah to discover the source of Samson's strength.

chunks.title: Samson Captured
chunks.reason: The climax of the Delilah narrative where Samson is finally shaved, his strength leaves him, and he is blinded and imprisoned.

chunks.title: Samson's Final Act
chunks.reason: A complete dramatic sequence covering the Philistine sacrifice, Samson's prayer for strength, and his death by collapsing the temple.

chunks.title: Samson's Burial
chunks.reason: A concluding narrative unit describing Samson's burial and the summary of his judgeship.

chunks.title: Micah's Idolatry and the State of Israel
chunks.reason: Narrative establishing Micah's background, his mother's blessing, the creation of idols, and the thematic statement about the lack of a king.

chunks.title: The Levite Joins Micah
chunks.reason: Narrative describing the arrival of the wandering Levite, his hiring by Micah, and Micah's conclusion that Yahweh will prosper him.

chunks.title: Danites Seek Land and Priest
chunks.reason: Introduction of the tribe of Dan seeking territory and their encounter with Micah's Levite priest.

chunks.title: Scouts Report on Laish
chunks.reason: The five spies discover the security of Laish and report back to encourage the tribe.

chunks.title: Danites March to Ephraim
chunks.reason: The main force of 600 men sets out and arrives at Micah's house.

chunks.title: The Priest is Recruited
chunks.reason: The spies reveal the idols to their kin and persuade the Levite priest to join them.

chunks.title: Flight from Micah
chunks.reason: The Danites flee with the idols and priest while Micah confronts them but yields to their strength.

chunks.title: Conquest of Laish and Establishment of Dan
chunks.reason: The tribe destroys Laish, rebuilds it as Dan, and establishes the idolatrous priesthood.

chunks.title: The Levite's Journey Begins
chunks.reason: Introduces the characters and the initial journey to retrieve the concubine.

chunks.title: Arrival in Gibeah
chunks.reason: Describes the travelers' arrival at night and their reception by an old man.

chunks.title: The Vile Men Attack
chunks.reason: Details the mob's arrival and the abuse of the concubine.

chunks.title: The Levite's Reaction
chunks.reason: Concludes with the discovery of the body and the cutting of the concubine.

chunks.title: Assembly at Mizpah and the Levite's Report
chunks.reason: The gathering of Israel and the Levite's detailed account of the crime in Gibeah.

chunks.title: Israel's Vow and Demand to Benjamin
chunks.reason: The unified response of Israel to the report and their ultimatum to the tribe of Benjamin.

chunks.title: Mobilization and Troop Counts
chunks.reason: The gathering of Benjamite forces and the numerical listing of soldiers from both sides.

chunks.title: First Two Battles and Divine Inquiry
chunks.reason: The first two days of battle where Israel suffers defeat but seeks God's guidance each time.

chunks.title: Third Day Strategy and Victory
chunks.reason: The final day of battle involving the ambush, the smoke signal, and the decisive defeat of Benjamin.

chunks.title: Pursuit and Destruction of Gibeah
chunks.reason: The rout of the Benjamites, the slaughter at Rimmon, and the burning of the city.

chunks.title: The Oath and the Crisis
chunks.reason: Describes the oath at Mizpah and the Israelites' distress over the missing tribe of Benjamin.

chunks.title: War on Jabesh-gilead
chunks.reason: Narrates the search for a tribe that missed the oath and the subsequent slaughter of Jabesh-gilead to find wives.

chunks.title: The Shiloh Solution
chunks.reason: Details the elders' plan for the remaining Benjamites to seize wives during a feast at Shiloh.

chunks.title: Conclusion and Epilogue
chunks.reason: Summarizes the dispersal of the tribes and states the theological observation about the lack of a king.
